steps.title: For tissue clearing, samples are infiltrated...
steps.instruction: For tissue clearing, samples are infiltrated with SHIELD polyepoxy (PMID: 30556815) for 6 days with constant agitation at 4 C, were polymerized at 37 oC for 24 hours, and were then delipidated using a SmartBatch+ ETC active tissue clearing platform (LifeCanvasTechnologies, Cambridge, MA).

steps.title: Transfer samples in a glass dish/vial...
steps.instruction: Transfer samples in a glass dish/vial after tissue clearing is done, since DMSO in the subsequent steps will dissolve plastic vessels.

steps.title: Wash with PBS 1X with 0
steps.instruction: Wash with PBS 1X with 0.2% Triton-X100, 2 times for 1 hour each at room temp.

steps.title: Block tissues with PBS 1X with 0
steps.instruction: Block tissues with PBS 1X with 0.2% Triton X-100, 10% DMSO, and 6% donkey serum for 1 day at 37°C. Use the incubator shaker at MMS8 next to the dissecting microscope (with gentle rocking ≈ 85 rpm).

steps.title: Wash with PBS 1X with 0
steps.instruction: Wash with PBS 1X with 0.2% Tween-20, and 10ug/mL heparin, 2 times for 1 hour each at 37°C.

steps.title: Bring tissue to microscopy core in...
steps.instruction: Bring tissue to microscopy core in PBS for refractive index matching and imaging.

steps.title: Wash with PBS 1X with 0
steps.instruction: Wash with PBS 1X with 0.2% Tween-20 and 10 ug/mL heparin for at least 5 times for 1 hour each at room temp on a rocker or gentle shaker. Thorough washing is essential to reducing background fluorescence.

steps.title: Counter-Stain with DAPI/Hoechst on day 3
steps.instruction: Counter-Stain with DAPI/Hoechst on day 3. Add DAPI/Hoechst (1:10000 dilution) to the staining solution.

steps.title: Stain with conjugated antibodies in the dark except for DAPI
steps.instruction: Stain with conjugated antibodies in the dark except for DAPI. Antibodies are used at a 1:200 dilution with PBS with 0.2% Tween-20, 5% DMSO, 3% donkey serum, and 10 ug/mL heparin at 37°C for 4 days on a rocker or gentle shaker (≈ 85 rpm). You can stain with up to 4 conjugated-Abs with different fluorophores with excitation peaks near the laser’s wavelengths (405, 488, 561, 640), or 3 conjugated-Abs (488, 561, 640) along with nuclear staining (to be added next step).

steps.title: Each tissue of interest is fixed...
steps.instruction: Each tissue of interest is fixed in 4% Paraformaldehyde for 24 hours at room temperature.

steps.title: Place the tissue in PBS and...
steps.instruction: Place the tissue in PBS and transfer it to the microscopy core for tissue clearing.

steps.title: Samples were washed in 1x PBS...
steps.instruction: Samples were washed in 1x PBS for 24 hours before proceeding to antibody staining,
